correct_explanation: irregular global memory accesses reduce load efficiency and make memory traffic dominate runtime
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: latency-bound

REASONING: The kernel is using almost none of peak DRAM bandwidth (0.01%) and only a small fraction of peak FLOPS, so it is neither memory-bandwidth-bound nor compute-bound. With high achieved occupancy (82.7%) but a very large long-scoreboard stall rate (44.73%), the dominant issue is waiting on memory/data dependencies, i.e. latency rather than throughput.
parsed_reasoning: The kernel is using almost none of peak DRAM bandwidth (0.01%) and only a small fraction of peak FLOPS, so it is neither memory-bandwidth-bound nor compute-bound. With high achieved occupancy (82.7%) but a very large long-scoreboard stall rate (44.73%), the dominant issue is waiting on memory/data dependencies, i.e. latency rather than throughput.
